items.reviewer_position: The unknown-host fallback assigns cross_provider to the codex backend, knowingly overstating independence in exactly the undeclared-non-Claude-host case the change exists to fix; the tests lock the unsafe fallback in.
items.host_position: Verified — and conceded: this was my deliberate backward-compatibility choice, but it is fail-open, and the repo's own precedent (unknown environment refuses self-review) is fail-safe. A positive independence claim requires both sides to be attributable.

verification.detail: independence_tier's final return assigned cross_provider for backend_name == 'codex' on every unmatched host, including 'unknown'; detect_host has no non-Claude auto-detection.

items.resolution: Unknown/unrecognized hosts now yield 'undetermined' with a notice telling the driver to set IMPASSE_HOST; the tests now pin that an unattributed host never receives a positive cross-provider claim (e2e included). The legacy suite pins IMPASSE_HOST=claude explicitly instead of relying on the fallback.

items.reviewer_position: detect_host derives identity from detect_environment, so IMPASSE_ENV=claude_code alone manufactures a Claude host and with it an unearned cross_provider label.
items.host_position: Verified: detect_environment honors IMPASSE_ENV before real markers, and detect_host mapped any Claude surface result to host=claude. A surface-policy override must not become an identity override.

verification.detail: detect_environment returns the forced IMPASSE_ENV value first; detect_host called it and mapped claude_code/chat_sandbox/cowork to 'claude'.
New regression test: with IMPASSE_ENV=claude_code and no genuine markers, detect_environment() == 'claude_code' but detect_host() == 'unknown' (passes post-fix).

items.resolution: detect_host now inspects the genuine Claude env markers directly and deliberately does not consult detect_environment; documented in the docstring and docs/environments.md.

items.reviewer_position: review_mode ranks nominal providers, ignoring configured base URLs and backend configurations get_backend would refuse (claude under Bedrock/Vertex), so the pre-flight can report a tier or recommend a backend the actual run contradicts.
items.host_position: Verified: candidates were hardcoded ('codex','OpenAI')/('claude','Anthropic') and availability came only from binary resolution.

verification.detail: review_mode built candidates from constant provider labels; the mode CLI's availability check used resolve_*_command only.

items.resolution: review_mode now computes each backend's provider from its configured endpoint env var (malformed/custom endpoints degrade to undetermined, mirroring the run) and excludes claude when CLAUDE_CODE_USE_BEDROCK/VERTEX is set. Tests cover both (gateway endpoint -> undetermined; Bedrock -> claude never recommended).

items.reviewer_position: review_mode returned notice=null for same_provider and undetermined selections while the docs claim every step down is disclosed; mode is an independently consumed CLI surface.
items.host_position: Verified: every subprocess-candidate return hardcoded notice None, delegating to review() — but the mode CLI is its own disclosure surface.

verification.detail: review_mode's candidate return set 'notice': None for all tiers; docs/environments.md promised disclosure for same_provider and undetermined.

items.resolution: Extracted one shared lib.independence_notice(tier, host, backend, provider) formatter used by both review() and review_mode(); the pre-flight now carries the notice for downgraded tiers. Tests assert notice presence for same_provider/undetermined selections and absence for cross_provider.

items.reviewer_position: CLAUDE_CODE_RECOMMENDATION still claims only Claude Code can run a cross-provider reviewer, and the review CLI help still labels codex 'cross-provider' statically — both contradict the host-relative policy.
items.host_position: Verified: the constant and the --backend/--effort help text (and review()'s docstring) carried Claude-host-static framing.

verification.detail: CLAUDE_CODE_RECOMMENDATION asserted 'only there can it run a cross-provider reviewer (Codex)'; --backend help said 'codex (cross-provider, default) or claude (same-provider fallback)'.

items.resolution: Recommendation is now host-aware: Claude(-ish) hosts get a reworded Claude Code recommendation; non-Claude hosts get a capability-framed SUBPROCESS_RECOMMENDATION. CLI help and review()'s docstring now state independence is host-relative. Tests pin the recommendation gating both ways.

items.reviewer_position: The tests verify the happy-path classification table but not the configuration interactions capable of producing false independence: IMPASSE_ENV inference, invalid hosts, custom endpoints, mode notices, refused backends, unknown-host disclosure.
items.host_position: Verified — and largely subsumed by the fixes for F001–F005, each of which landed with its own regression test.

verification.detail: Suite grew to 190 checks: IMPASSE_ENV-only inference, unrecognized IMPASSE_HOST, unknown-host e2e disclosure, gateway-endpoint pre-flight, Bedrock exclusion, notice presence/absence per tier, and recommendation gating all pass.

items.resolution: Added the adversarial cases enumerated in F001–F005 as regression tests; the unknown-fallback test now asserts the fail-safe behavior instead of enshrining the fail-open one.
